title: The Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Ordinance, 1962 (East Pakistan Ordinance)
title_en: The Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Ordinance, 1962 (East Pakistan Ordinance)
description: An Ordinance for the constitution of the Bangladesh Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals. WHEREAS it is expedient to constitute a [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, for the purpose and in the manner hereinafter appearing; NOW, THEREFORE, in pursuance of the Presidential Proclamation of the seventh day of October, 1958, and in exercise of all powers enabling him in that behalf, th...

provisions.title: 1. Short title, extent and commencement
provisions.content:
1. (1) This Ordinance may be called the [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Ordinance, 1962.
(2) It extends to the whole of Bangladesh.
(3) It shall come into force at once.

provisions.title: 2. Definitions
provisions.content:
2. In this Ordinance, unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context,-
(a) “animal” means any domestic or captured animal except poultry;
(b) “Branch Society” means the Branch of the [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals for a district constituted under section 11; and
(c) “Society” means the [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals constituted under section 3.

provisions.title: 3. Constitution of the 5 [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals
provisions.content:
3. (1) There shall be constituted a Society to be known as “the [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to animals”.
(2) [The President of the People's Republic of Bangladesh] shall be the Chairman of the Society and the first members thereof shall be appointed either by name or by office by the Government.
(3) The headquarters of the Society shall be at Dacca.

provisions.title: 4. Incorporation
provisions.content: 4. The first members of the Society and all persons who may hereafter become members thereof so long as they continue so to be, shall be a body corporate under the name of the [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, and the said body shall have perpetual succession and a common seal with powers to hold and acquire property, movable or immovable, and shall sue and be sued by the said name.

provisions.title: 5. Functions of the Society
6. Appointment of Executive Committee
provisions.content:
6. (1) Until such time as an Executive Committee of the Society is constituted in accordance with the regulations made under section 7, the Executive Committee shall consist of such persons from among the members of the Society as may be nominated by the Government.
(2) The Government may nominate as office-bearers of the first Executive Committee such persons as it may select for the purpose:
Provided that the Government may reconstitute the first Executive Committee as and when it is deemed necessary.

provisions.title: 7. Power to make regulation
provisions.content:
7. The first Executive Committee shall, subject to the previous approval of the Government, make regulations for the management, control and procedure of the Society. Such regulations may, among other matters, provide for the following, namely:-
(a) the conditions of membership of the Society;
(b) the constitution and functions of the Executive Committee; and
(c) the regulation of the procedure generally of the Society and the Executive Committee.

provisions.title: 8. [Repealed]
provisions.content: 8. [Dissolution and transfer of property of the E.P. Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, Dacca.- Repealed by section 3 and the Second Schedule of the Bangladesh Laws (Revision And Declaration) Act, 1973 (Act No. VIII of 1973).]

provisions.title: 9. Fund
10. Purposes to which the funds of the society may be applied
11. Branch Society
provisions.content:
11. (1) There shall be a branch of the [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals for every district [* * *].
(2) The Deputy Commissioner of the district shall be the Chairman of the Branch Society, and the first members thereof shall be appointed either by name or by office by the Deputy Commissioner.

provisions.title: 12. [Repealed]
provisions.content: 12. [Distribution of income among Branch Societies.- Repealed by section 3 and the Second Schedule of the Bangladesh Laws (Revision And Declaration) Act, 1973 (Act No. VIII of 1973).]

provisions.title: 13. Decision of Executive Committee as to purposes final
provisions.content: 13. The Executive Committee of the Society shall have authority to determine in all cases what matters properly fall within the scope of section 10, and its decision in all such matters shall be binding on all the Branch Societies.

provisions.title: 14. Receipt and use of gifts
provisions.content: 14. The Executive Committee of the Society may also receive and hold gifts of whatever description either for the general purposes of the Society, and [* * *] on receipt of such gifts, may, subject to such conditions as may be prescribed, apply the same to such purposes either directly or through the Branch Societies.

provisions.title: 15. Powers of Branch Societies
provisions.content:
15. Subject to the provisions of this Ordinance and the rules made thereunder, and subject to the regulations of the Society made under section 7, each Branch Society shall have all powers to regulate its own procedure and constitution, to receive gifts and donations, and to expend all monies received by it for its purposes:
Provided that 10 per cent of cash donations received by any Branch Society for any of the objects set out in section 10 shall be placed at the disposal of the Executive Committee of the Society to be applied by it for the general purposes of the Society.

provisions.title: 16. Audit
provisions.content:
16. (1) The accounts of the Society and of the Branch Societies shall be audited annually by an Auditor to be appointed by the Executive Committee of the Society with the prior approval of the Government.
(2) The Society and the Branch Societies shall produce all accounts, books and connected documents and furnish such other information and explanations as the Auditor or any officer authorised by him in this behalf may require at the time of the audit.
(3) A statement of accounts audited by the Auditor referred to in sub section (1), and containing such particulars as may be prescribed by rules shall be furnished to the Government as soon as possible after the end of every financial year.
(4) The Government shall examine the Auditor's Report and take such action thereon as it may deem fit.

provisions.title: 17. Power to make rules

definitions.definition: any domestic or captured animal except poultry
the Branch of the [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals for a district constituted under section 11
the [* * *] Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals constituted under section 3.
